qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Pkcs12Ref::parse2 panics on interior-NUL passwords
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an interior-NUL panic in `openssl/src/pkcs12.rs` caused by `CString::new(pass.as_bytes()).unwrap()`, not the advisory's missing output-length check in `openssl/src/md_ctx.rs::digest_final()` before `EVP_DigestFinal`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is the missing size check in `MdCtxRef::digest_final()` that allows `EVP_DigestFinal()` to write past an undersized caller buffer, while the session's only qualifying finding is a separate PKCS#12 password-to-`CString` panic.
evidence: The advisory summary and patch reference localize the vulnerability to `openssl/src/md_ctx.rs::digest_final()`, where commit `826c3888b77add418b394770e2b2e3a72d9f92fe` added `if self.size() > len as usize { return Err(ErrorStack::get()); }` before `EVP_DigestFinal`.
The session manifest lists exactly one real final qualifying finding: `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_d1b85c9e/pkcs12_parse2_nul_password_panic`.
That finding's report and reproduced PoC target `openssl/src/pkcs12.rs:52`, where `Pkcs12Ref::parse2` unwraps `CString::new(pass.as_bytes())` and panics on interior-NUL passwords.
The PKCS#12 panic does not share the advisory's vulnerable function, file, sink, or root-cause mechanism, so it remains an open-world finding rather than an advisory match.
